behavior_summary: U8P_ARMRIGHT::use (process type 0x0207) is invoked when the body-part object is interacted with; it unconditionally spawns a BODYPART helper process (BODYPART::func0270) with a single 0 parameter to start the part's visual/behavioral routine.

U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit (process type 0x020B, params: uword, word) handles U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit events for the body-part: it calls FREE::resetRef(0x0202, this) to clear an internal reference, then queries Kernel::getNumProcesses(0x020A, this). If that count is zero, it spawns a BODYPART U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-reaction process (BODYPART::func0431) with parameter 0.
No global flags are read or written by these routines; behavior is driven by spawning helper processes and by checking/clearing internal references.
Local context: 'this' is the body-part/NPC instance; 'temp' holds spawn return/handle values.
notable_control_flow: U8P_ARMRIGHT::use performs a single external action (spawn BODYPART::func0270) and then ends — there are no conditionals or flag changes.

U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit always invokes FREE::resetRef first, then conditionally spawns BODYPART::func0431 only when Kernel::getNumProcesses reports zero for process type 0x020A for this object (guard to avoid duplicate U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-reaction processes).
open_questions: What exact visuals/logic are implemented in BODYPART::func0270 and BODYPART::func0431 (spawned processes)? (inferred: initialization and U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-reaction/animation respectively)
What semantic purpose does the reference index 0x0202 used in FREE::resetRef serve for this object?
What do the declared parameters (uword, word) of U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit represent (e.g., damage type, source id, U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit flags)?
Is the spawn return value ('temp') used elsewhere or retained by the caller environment after spawn?

main_function.name: U8P_ARMRIGHT::use
main_function.purpose: Start the body-part's helper process/behavior when the player interacts with the right-arm body-part by spawning BODYPART::func0270 tied to this object.
entry_conditions: Triggered by an interaction/U8P_ARMRIGHT::use event on the body-part object (process type 0x0207, inferred OnUse).

function_map.name: U8P_ARMRIGHT::use
function_map.role: Start/initialize the body-part helper process on player interaction (spawns BODYPART::func0270).

function_map.name: U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit
function_map.role: Handle U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit events: clear internal reference and conditionally spawn a U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-reaction helper process (BODYPART::func0431) if none active.

function_map.name: BODYPART::func0270
function_map.role: Spawned helper process likely implementing the body-part's visual/behavior routine (initialized by U8P_ARMRIGHT::use).

function_map.name: BODYPART::func0431
function_map.role: Spawned helper process likely implementing U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-reaction/animation (invoked by U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit).

function_map.name: FREE::resetRef
function_map.role: Clear or reset an internal reference/slot for the object (used as cleanup/prep before handling a U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit).

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Query number of running processes of a given type for this object (used as a guard against duplicate U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-react processes).

external_calls.purpose: Start the BODYPART helper/process to initialize visual/behavioral behavior for the body-part when used.
Clear an internal reference/slot (argument 0x0202, this) as part of U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit handling/cleanup.
Check how many processes of type 0x020A exist for this object to decide whether to spawn a U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-reaction process.
Start a U8P_ARMRIGHT::hit-reaction helper/process when no existing 0x020A-type processes are running for this object.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_ARMRIGHT::use (process type 0x0207) is invoked when the body-part object is interacted with; it unconditionally spawns a BODYPART helper process (BODYPART::func0270) with a single 0 parameter to start the part's visual/behavioral routine.
